title: Allow users to set OS and architecture on bud
body:
When building images we now allow the architecture and operating system
to be overwritten via the new `--os` and `--arch` flags. This allows the
use case of packing pre-built or cross-compiled binaries into container
images with specifying the correct environment. The `--platform` flag
now takes the `os/arch` format into account as well.

Inspired by: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/87325
cc @vrothberg 

As a follow up we could bring that functionality to podman, too.

comments.body: FWIW `buildah config` supports setting both via `--arch` and `--os`, which somehow dwarfs my argument against support something similar to `--os` for `buildah bud`.

> Thanks for tackling that, @saschagrunert!
> 
> `--override-{arch,os}` are usually used to control image pulling, so I object to re-purposing the two options for setting certain fields in an image config.
> 
> `--override-arch` may be `--platform` which is currently a NOP but the correct option to remain compatible with Docker.

The thing is that we have `--arch` and `--os` in the `manifest add` subcommand as well. We could also add those two to the `bud` command and remove the `--override-*` commands for them (not sure about deprecation policy for hidden commands). So I see three options:

1. leave it as it currently is implemented
1. use `--platform` for `--override-arch` and still use `--override-os`
1. add `--arch`/`--os` and remove `--override-*` flags

> In case of `--override-os`, I don't think we need to expose a way to change the OS. I don't know of any way to do cross-OS builds.

cri-tools cross compiles to windows as well for example: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cri-tools/blob/47a7405313727fe8875328a46bfa53594a211e2c/hack/release.sh#L57-L66

> > Thanks for tackling that, @saschagrunert!
> > `--override-{arch,os}` are usually used to control image pulling, so I object to re-purposing the two options for setting certain fields in an image config.
> > `--override-arch` may be `--platform` which is currently a NOP but the correct option to remain compatible with Docker.
> 
> The thing is that we have `--arch` and `--os` in the `manifest add` subcommand as well. We could also add those two to the `bud` command and remove the `--override-*` commands for them (not sure about deprecation policy for hidden commands). So I see three options:
> 
>     1. leave it as it currently is implemented
>     2. use `--platform` for `--override-arch` and still use `--override-os`

`--override-*` is used for pulling images. I think the `manifest` sub-commands are a good point but an inconsistency. The `--override-*` options could still be used to control which image is being pulled during `bud`.

>     3. add `--arch`/`--os` and remove `--override-*` flags

I think that's the way to go. It seems that `--platform` combines `--arch` and `--os` and eats input such as `linux/amd64`, so we can easily support that as well.

> > In case of `--override-os`, I don't think we need to expose a way to change the OS. I don't know of any way to do cross-OS builds.
> 
> cri-tools cross compiles to windows as well for example: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cri-tools/blob/47a7405313727fe8875328a46bfa53594a211e2c/hack/release.sh#L57-L66

I meant cross-container builds not cross compilation. We don't support building/running images for any other OS than Linux, so I doubt the `--os` flag would be useful but as other commands expose it, it doesn't hurt to provide it for `bud` as well.

I would prefer to stick with the short names.  We have a way of aliasing the longer names to the short that is used in libpod.
```
	flags.SetNormalizeFunc(aliasFlags)
func aliasFlags(f *pflag.FlagSet, name string) pflag.NormalizedName {
	switch name {
	case "healthcheck-command":
		name = "health-cmd"
	case "healthcheck-interval":
		name = "health-interval"
	case "healthcheck-retries":
		name = "health-retries"
	case "healthcheck-start-period":
		name = "health-start-period"
	case "healthcheck-timeout":
		name = "health-timeout"
	case "net":
		name = "network"
	}
	return pflag.NormalizedName(name)
}
```

@rhatdan, we are not targetting aliasing. The problem is that `--override-arch` is commonly used to control which image is being pulled. In the current state of the PR it would control which image is being built.

So we don't want to alias but to iron out the ambiguity of `--override-arch` and `--arch` across the tools and (sub) commands. I want it to be as consistent as possible and believe that `--arch` and `--os` are the right names for setting the corresponding fields in the image config.

> Command completions are missing.

Fixed that as well :+1:
Two open discussion points left, one related to the system context seems critical because its currently breaking the behavior.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 0c3234f) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

:pushpin: Commit 54cae15 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 54cae15fbce262c0493daa3ec3ed8b09cd83f41f -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
Happy to bring this into podman as well after a new buildah release.

> Happy to bring this into podman as well after a new buildah release.

any idea when this will be ? thank you!

Short Answer: @Prashanth684 soon.

Longer Answer: @rhatdan is working on getting Podman to deal with the new containers.conf file (https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4698).  The work necessary for Buildah to do that just wrapped up (#1231) so I suspect Podman will shortly too.  Once that's all complete we'll create  new release for Buildah and will vendor that into Podman.  I'm hoping later this week, early next, but no promises.
thanks @TomSweeneyRedHat . No worries. Just wanted to know.
